message: Sometimes I want to breathe in some fresh air too.
In the early morning, Central Park is bustling with joggers and dog walkers, but it's sparse during the day.
This area is on the edge of the vast Central Park. Regular folks usually only come this far.
The slightly chilly weather seems to have kept even sunbathers and outdoor lunchers away.
They don't seem like the type to enjoy walking or forest bathing. In fact, being awake at this time of day seems out of character.
I don't know what you were doing here, but you don't seem awkward about meeting me.
You look like a perfectly normal gentleman, quite a change from when we met at your house.
Thanks for having me the other day.
About the time I visited your home.
You called Gabriela and then came to see me, remember?
Now that you mention it, I seem to recall something like that.
I wonder if I said something rude?
It was a bit tricky, the conversation didn't seem to flow well.
My apologies. I really am sorry. Go on, ask me anything. It's my way of making up for that.
No, wait a minute. I had plans from now on. Let's change the place and talk.
I'll be waiting in front of
